evals.expected_output: Controller asks 3 questions in one message: (1) repo paths, (2) additional domains with 9 defaults listed, (3) scan depth. Does NOT dispatch or start analysis without repo paths.

assertions.text: Response asks for repo path(s) (not just repo names)
Response lists all 9 default analysis domains
Response asks about scan depth and explains quick vs deep
All 3 questions are in one message
Does NOT start analysis without repo paths

evals.prompt: コードベースを全部レビューして改善点を洗い出してほしい。$HOME/projects/project-a だけでいい。quickで頼む。
evals.expected_output: Controller recognizes path=$HOME/projects/project-a and depth=quick. Either asks only about additional domains (the one unknown param), or proceeds directly to analysis. Does NOT ask for params already specified.

assertions.text: Controller recognizes $HOME/projects/project-a as the target path
Controller recognizes quick as the scan depth
Controller does NOT re-ask for path or depth
Controller either starts analysis or only asks about additional domains

evals.prompt:
/codebase-audit

パス: $HOME/projects/project-a, $HOME/projects/project-b
追加領域: パフォーマンス・スケーラビリティ設計
深度: deep
evals.expected_output: All params provided. Controller begins analysis (or confirms and begins). Analysis uses the A×B framework with パフォーマンス・スケーラビリティ設計 as domain 10, depth=deep. Output format follows the markdown template with per-repo sections + cross-repo analysis.

assertions.text: パフォーマンス・スケーラビリティ設計 is included as an additional domain
Output follows the structured report format with domain sections and [評価軸][優先度] tags
